reasoning: All four correctly diagnose post hoc reasoning, the revenue-per-employee/productivity equivocation, the headcount-denominator effect, the uncontrolled competitor comparison, and the non-sequitur leap to a five-day mandate, all closing with pilot-before-mandate recommendations. D edges ahead by adding the falsifiability/hard-to-vary test (Flaw 6: the story explains either outcome) and the explicit 2x2 competitor cell structure, plus the sharpest base-rate framing (12% over six years is ~2%/yr, possibly above-sector) and the cleanest argument map. C is nearly identical in quality with the strong 'semi-attached figure' and false-dilemma naming and an explicit premortem, but lacks D's unfalsifiability angle. A is thorough and well-organized (ten itemized flaws, good reversibility point) but more list-like and slightly less analytically tight; B is solid and clear with a good counterfactual/diff-in-diff point but reads as a flatter enumeration with the least distinctive insight and weakest depth.
